stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's latest large language model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's latest LLM releases.

stories.title: This could be Windows’ M1 moment — but expect it to cost a ton
stories.author: Antonio G. Di Benedetto
bullet_points: Nvidia is entering the consumer laptop chip market with its RTX Spark technology.
The announcement is significant as it challenges the dominance of established architectures in Windows laptops.
The article frames this as a potential 'M1 moment' for Windows, contrasting with Apple's successful Arm implementation.

stories.title: Meta’s own AI was exploited to hijack Instagram accounts
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Meta's AI support chatbot was exploited by hackers to compromise Instagram accounts.
The attack involved tricking the chatbot into changing the associated email address on a target's profile.
This vulnerability allows attackers to subsequently reset the password and take over the account.

stories.title: Anthropic has officially filed to go public
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Anthropic has officially filed with the SEC to begin its Initial Public Offering (IPO).
This filing marks a significant milestone in the highly anticipated race between major AI players (like OpenAI and Anthropic) to go public.
The event is positioned to be a massive financial and industry event given Anthropic's current valuation and market presence.

stories.title: Dozens of Red Hat packages backdoored through its official NPM channel
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Dozens of Red Hat packages were compromised via its official NPM channel.
This constitutes a significant security vulnerability affecting users who downloaded the affected packages.
Immediate investigation is advised for anyone who has downloaded the compromised Red Hat packages.

stories.title: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman after multiple ChatGPT-linked murders
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Florida is suing OpenAI and Sam Altman following multiple alleged murders linked to ChatGPT.
The lawsuit includes allegations that Altman demonstrates an 'utter disregard' for human lives.
This represents a significant legal and ethical challenge to the deployment and safety of large language models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation and utilizes transformers and safetensors formats.
The model is associated with the deepseek_v3 family and is suitable for conversational tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon devices, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The update is currently in preview, signaling a push for optimized local AI on Apple hardware.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and control.
The core value proposition is connecting messaging workflows directly to local, private AI capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is partnering with OpenAI and ROOST to deploy gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: Hackers duped Meta AI support chatbot to steal celebrity Instagram accounts
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Hackers exploited a vulnerability in Meta's AI support chatbot.
The exploit allowed unauthorized access to and theft of celebrity Instagram accounts.
The stolen, high-value Instagram handles were subsequently resold before Meta patched the flaw.

stories.title: From 15 hours to one minute: How AI/ML is speeding up GM's development
stories.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
bullet_points: General Motors (GM) is leveraging AI/ML to drastically accelerate vehicle development timelines.
The shift involves extensive virtualization, utilizing technologies like CFD (Computational Fluid Dynamics) and FEA (Finite Element Analysis).
The adoption of digital twins is a key component in modernizing the automotive design and testing process.

stories.title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
stories.author: Hyunjung Shim
bullet_points: Identifies 'Perceptual Judgment Bias' in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge, where MLLMs favor plausible narratives over visually accurate answers.
The bias occurs when visual evidence contradicts textual cues during automated evaluation.
The research proposes using perceptual perturbation and reward modeling to mitigate this reliability issue in MLLM evaluators.

stories.title: AdaCodec: A Predictive Visual Code for Video MLLMs
stories.author: Jiaqi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces AdaCodec, a novel predictive visual coding method for video.
Addresses the inefficiency of current Video MLLMs which encode every frame independently, leading to redundant visual tokens.
Proposes a system where full reference frames are only sent when prediction fails, otherwise transmitting compact inter-frame descriptions.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating a focus on large language models.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance to modern deep learning frameworks.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports experimental local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model runners and proprietary API services.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Gemini’s new AI agent is about as good as Google’s demo
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Google has introduced a new AI agent called Gemini Spark, advertised as a 24/7 task-handling assistant.
The reviewer found the agent to be 'shockingly good' at performing tasks.
The article raises concerns regarding the financial cost and potential privacy implications of using Spark.

stories.title: AI costs how much? GitHub Copilot users react to new usage-based pricing system.
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: GitHub Copilot is implementing a new usage-based pricing model for its AI features.
Early user reactions indicate that high usage can quickly exhaust monthly credit allotments.
The story addresses the practical financial implications of rapidly scaling AI tool usage.

stories.title: Moderna gets $50 million to develop mRNA Ebola vaccine against Bundibugyo
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: Moderna is receiving $50 million in funding.
The development focuses on an mRNA vaccine against Ebola.
The vaccine targets the Bundibugyo strain amid an ongoing outbreak.

stories.title: Microsoft's Surface Laptop Ultra looks like its first true MacBook Pro competitor
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: Microsoft's Surface Laptop Ultra is positioned as a serious competitor to the MacBook Pro.
The device is described as Microsoft's most refined effort in the high-end mobile workstation category.
The article suggests it represents a significant step toward a less 'weird' high-end Windows offering.

stories.title: ClinEnv: An Interactive Multi-Stage Long Horizon EHR Environment for Agents
stories.author: May Dongmei Wang
bullet_points: ClinEnv introduces a new interactive benchmark for evaluating LLMs in clinical settings.
It simulates real inpatient admissions, moving beyond static, multiple-choice medical benchmarks.
The environment tests an agent's ability to make sequential, irreversible decisions while gathering heterogeneous information under uncertainty.

stories.title: Permissive Safety Through Trusted Inference: Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters for Assured Interactive Robotics
stories.author: Haimin Hu
bullet_points: The research proposes 'Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters' for autonomous robots.
The goal is to ensure safety in interactive robotics despite human uncertainty (preferences, goals, cooperation levels).
Safety filters are highlighted as a modular approach to separate safety constraints from task performance.

stories.title: From Layers to Submodules: Rethinking Granularity in Replacement-Based LLM Compression
stories.author: Giovanni Iacca
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in current LLM compression techniques, specifically the reliance on full-layer granularity and contiguous component selection.
It proposes rethinking the granularity of replacement-based compression, suggesting that redundancy in transformers is not limited to contiguous blocks.
The research implies that compression strategies should account for uneven redundancy distribution between Attention and FeedForward network outputs.

stories.title: SimSD: Simple Speculative Decoding in Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Jingbo Shang
bullet_points: Introduces SimSD, a method to apply speculative decoding to Diffusion Language Models (dLLMs).
Addresses a key limitation: the masked language modeling formulation of dLLMs is incompatible with standard token-level speculative decoding used in Autoregressive (AR) LLMs.
Aims to accelerate dLLM inference, leveraging the speed benefits of diffusion models while incorporating AR-style acceleration techniques.

stories.title: SafeSteer: Localized On-Policy Distillation for Efficient Safety Alignment
stories.author: Lei Sha
bullet_points: Addresses the 'alignment tax' in LLMs, where safety alignment degrades general capabilities.
Proposes SafeSteer, a method for localized, on-policy distillation to achieve safety alignment.
Argues that safety modifications should be localized due to the sparse nature of safety features, avoiding global trade-offs.

stories.title: ProtoAda: Prototype-Guided Adaptive Adapter Expansion and Geometric Consolidation for Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
stories.author: Da-Wei Zhou
bullet_points: The paper addresses the need for Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT) to allow MLLMs to learn new vision-language capabilities over time.
It proposes 'ProtoAda,' a method utilizing Prototype-Guided Adaptive Adapter Expansion and Geometric Consolidation to improve this tuning.
The research tackles the challenge of inter-task interference, a common issue when adapting large models to sequential learning tasks.

stories.title: IntraShuffler: A Privacy Preserving Framework for Heterogeneous DP Federated Learning
stories.author: Jinyuan Stella Sun
bullet_points: Introduces IntraShuffler, a framework for Privacy Preserving Heterogeneous Differential Privacy (HDP) in Federated Learning (FL).
Addresses the challenge of non-IID data patterns in FL, which can compromise privacy guarantees.
Allows clients to use individualized privacy budgets ($\varepsilon_i$) based on institutional policies while aiming to improve model utility.

stories.title: Permissive Safety Through Trusted Inference: Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters for Assured Interactive Robotics
stories.author: Haimin Hu
bullet_points: The research proposes 'Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters' for autonomous robots.
These filters aim to ensure safety in interactive robotics when dealing with human uncertainty (preferences, goals, cooperation).
The approach builds upon the modular concept of safety filters, separating safety concerns from core task performance.

stories.title: Auditing Asset-Specific Preferences in Financial Large Language Models: Evidence from Bitcoin Representations and Portfolio Allocation
stories.author: Wenbin Wu
bullet_points: The research investigates potential asset-specific biases within Large Language Models (LLMs) used in financial applications like robo-advisors.
The study proposes a three-level audit protocol to test if LLMs systematically prefer certain financial instruments, using Bitcoin as a case study.
It aims to identify internal representations within LLMs that causally influence these asset preferences and downstream financial decisions.

stories.title: Drifting Preference Optimization for One-Step Generative Models
stories.author: Zhen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the difficulty of preference finetuning for fast, one-step text-to-image generators.
Introduces Drifting Preference Optimization (DrPO), a novel online preference-finetuning method.
DrPO is designed specifically for deterministic, single-forward-pass generative models, bypassing complex standard alignment requirements.

stories.title: Towards Automated Discovery: A Review of Generative Models, Multimodal Learning and Closed-Loop Workflows in Inverse Materials Design
stories.author: Gian-Marco Rignanese
bullet_points: The paper reviews the shift in materials discovery towards 'inverse materials design,' moving from simple prediction to targeted candidate proposal.
It surveys recent advancements in generative crystal structure modeling and multimodal learning for crystalline solids.
The review focuses on closed-loop design pipelines, detailing how modern generators learn chemical-structural priors for controllable sampling.

stories.title: Iteris: Agentic Research Loops for Computational Mathematics
stories.author: Bin Dong
bullet_points: Introduces 'Iteris,' an agentic research system designed to advance computational mathematics.
Addresses the gap where current AI progress often overlooks the need for numerical experimentation and adversarial construction in math research.
Leverages recent advances in LLMs and agentic AI to tackle complex mathematical problems and conjectures.

stories.title: AdaCodec: A Predictive Visual Code for Video MLLMs
stories.author: Jiaqi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces AdaCodec, a predictive visual code designed to address redundancy in video data for MLLMs.
Current video MLLMs inefficiently encode every frame independently, leading to redundant visual tokens.
AdaCodec proposes sending a full reference frame only when prediction fails, otherwise transmitting a compact inter-frame description.

stories.title: ClinEnv: An Interactive Multi-Stage Long Horizon EHR Environment for Agents
stories.author: May Dongmei Wang
bullet_points: Introduces ClinEnv, a new interactive, multi-stage environment designed to simulate real-world inpatient clinical practice.
Addresses the limitation of static benchmarks by modeling the sequential, uncertain decision-making process of physicians.
Aims to evaluate Large Language Models (LLMs) in the role of attending physicians over longitudinal patient admissions.

stories.title: From Layers to Submodules: Rethinking Granularity in Replacement-Based LLM Compression
stories.author: Giovanni Iacca
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in current LLM compression techniques, specifically focusing on replacement-based methods.
Existing methods are constrained by requiring full-layer granularity and contiguous component selection.
The research proposes that LLM redundancy is not strictly confined to contiguous regions, suggesting a need for finer-grained compression strategies.

stories.title: HERO'S JOURNEY: Testing Complex Rule Induction with Text Games
stories.author: Junyi Jessy Li
bullet_points: Introduces HERO'S JOURNEY, a new benchmark for testing rule induction in goal-directed, episodic tasks.
The benchmark tests agents' ability to infer hidden rules from demonstrations across various structural forms.
Evaluations of state-of-the-art LLMs show evidence of rule induction, but note limitations in current model capabilities.

stories.title: SN-WER: Script-Normalized WER for Multi-Script Indic ASR Evaluation
stories.author: Priyaranjan Pattnayak
bullet_points: Introduces SN-WER, a new metric to address inaccuracies in Word Error Rate (WER) for multi-script Indic ASR.
SN-WER resolves overestimation of errors when references and hypotheses use different scripts for the same words (e.g., romanization).
The method is training-free and evaluation-only, involving transliteration into a canonical script before WER calculation.

stories.title: Transferable Self-Harm Surveillance from Emergency Department Triage Notes Using an Evidence-Augmented Machine Learning Approach
stories.author: Vlada Rozova
bullet_points: The research addresses the public health challenge of self-harm surveillance, noting limitations in current diagnostic coding.
It proposes using Emergency Department (ED) triage notes as a high-potential source for early self-harm detection.
The methodology combines traditional machine learning with Large Language Model (LLM)-based screening and evidence extraction for enhanced accuracy.

stories.title: SimSD: Simple Speculative Decoding in Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Jingbo Shang
bullet_points: Introduces SimSD, a method to apply speculative decoding to Diffusion Language Models (dLLMs).
Addresses a key limitation: dLLMs' masked language modeling formulation is incompatible with standard token-level speculative decoding used in autoregressive (AR) models.
Aims to accelerate dLLM inference, leveraging the efficiency of speculative decoding techniques.

stories.title: SkillHarm: Lifecycle-Aware Skill-Based Attacks via Automated Construction
stories.author: Huan Sun
bullet_points: Introduces SkillHarm, a new benchmark for skill-based attacks targeting AI agents.
Addresses a gap in current research by evaluating attacks across the entire skill-use lifecycle, not just single tasks.
Highlights the vulnerability of third-party skills within agent workflows, which are implicitly executed by agents.

stories.title: SafeSteer: Localized On-Policy Distillation for Efficient Safety Alignment
stories.author: Lei Sha
bullet_points: Addresses the 'alignment tax' in LLMs, where safety alignment degrades general capabilities.
Proposes SafeSteer, a method for localized, on-policy distillation for efficient safety alignment.
Argues that safety modifications should be localized rather than relying on global trade-offs or massive auxiliary data.

stories.title: The newest Instagram “exploit” is the goofiest I've seen
stories.author: ssiddharth
bullet_points: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support bot to compromise Instagram accounts.
The exploit leverages the functionality of Meta's AI support system.
The story highlights a security vulnerability in AI-driven customer support tools.

stories.title: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
stories.author: typpo
bullet_points: OpenAI's frontier models are now accessible via AWS.
Codex, OpenAI's code generation model, is also available on the AWS platform.
This integration signifies a major shift in how enterprises can access cutting-edge AI infrastructure.

stories.title: Fixed Before Anyone Notices, Stronger After Every Fix: Self-Healing + Recurrence Prevention (Series Part 4)
stories.author: ryantsuji
bullet_points: Describes an advanced CI/CD pipeline featuring AI-driven self-healing capabilities.
The system automatically investigates production alerts, creates fixes (PRs), and deploys them.
It implements recurrence prevention by using the fix PR to introduce permanent quality gates (lint rules, CI guards) to block the same issue in the future.

stories.title: Why Your AI Agent needs better Temporal Reasoning—and How We Fixed It
stories.author: vektor_memory_43f51a32376
bullet_points: The article addresses a limitation in current AI agent memory systems: the lack of temporal reasoning.
Existing systems often store facts linearly, failing to account for when a fact was valid or true.
The content suggests a solution or improvement to address this temporal reasoning deficit in AI agents.
